text.title: Affinity Diagramming Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Affinity Diagramming application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: First clustering pass MUST be silent — no naming, no discussion. Groups emerge only after every card is placed.
rule.rationale: Pre-naming biases placement; silent sort produces honest groups.

rule.statement: Cluster labels are written AFTER the silent pass. Pre-existing labels are erased and re-derived from cards.
rule.rationale: Pre-existing labels recruit cards that don't belong, distorting the cluster shape.

rule.statement: Cards that don't fit any cluster go to a labelled 'orphan' bucket — never forced into the nearest cluster. Orphans ≥5% of total trigger re-clustering.
rule.rationale: Forced placement hides outlier signals; orphan rate is a quality metric.

rule.statement: Each cluster carries size (card count) and confidence (distinct sources × recency). Single-source clusters are tagged 'anecdotal'.
rule.rationale: Stakeholders default to high-confidence reading without explicit confidence bands.

rule.statement: Each cluster carries the segment distribution of its cards. Cross-segment clusters are flagged and reviewed for split.
rule.rationale: Cross-segment clusters merge incompatible jobs and dilute the theme.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
